learn.title: India's Solar Manufacturing Boost
learn.intro: India's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aims to boost domestic solar manufacturing, reducing import reliance and cutting costs. This policy drives job creation and supports the country's renewable energy goals, making solar power more accessible and affordable.
learn.c1_text: The PLI scheme offers financial incentives to manufacturers, encouraging domestic production. For every solar cell and module produced, companies receive a subsidy, reducing costs by up to 20%. This initiative aims to make India a global hub for solar manufacturing.
learn.c2_text: By 2024, the PLI scheme is expected to create 100,000+ jobs and attract $10 billion in investments. This growth supports India's target of 500 GW renewable energy by 2030, reducing dependence on imported solar panels.
learn.c3_text: India's solar manufacturing capacity is projected to reach 100 GW by 2025. The PLI scheme aligns with the National Solar Mission, aiming to make solar energy the cheapest source of power in the country.
learn.stat2: PLI scheme target: 100 GW domestic manufacturing by 2025

learn.title: HVDC Power Transmission
learn.intro: HVDC lines transmit electricity over long distances with minimal loss, enabling efficient power transfer from remote renewable sources. This technology is vital for integrating renewable energy into national grids.
learn.c1_text: HVDC uses direct current (DC) instead of alternating current (AC), reducing transmission losses by up to 30%. It's ideal for long-distance transmission, like from wind farms to cities.
learn.c2_text: HVDC can transmit power over 2,000 km with 50% less loss than AC. It also allows for better control and stability in power grids, especially with renewable sources.
learn.c3_text: India's first HVDC project, the 680 km Samagaon-Roorkee line, transmits 1,200 MW from hydropower. Future projects aim to connect solar and wind farms across the country.
learn.stat1: HVDC can transmit 1,000+ km with 30% less loss
learn.stat3: HVDC cost: 15-20% higher than AC, but 50% less loss

learn.title: Wind Turbine Lifecycle
learn.intro: Wind turbines last 20-25 years, requiring maintenance and eventual blade recycling. Proper end-of-life management is crucial for sustainable wind energy growth.
learn.c1_text: Modern turbines last 20-25 years, with regular maintenance extending their life. Blades, made of fiberglass, are durable but need recycling after use.
learn.c2_text: Turbines require annual inspections, blade repairs, and gearbox checks. Predictive maintenance using sensors can reduce downtime by 20%.
learn.c3_text: India's wind sector is developing recycling programs. The National Institute of Wind Energy (NIWE) is testing blade recycling methods, aiming to recover 90% of materials.
learn.stat4: Maintenance cost: 1-2% of total turbine cost

learn.title: Tidal Energy Power
learn.intro: Tidal stream energy harnesses ocean currents, offering predictable and reliable power. This technology is emerging as a key component of marine renewable energy.
learn.c1_text: Tidal stream turbines generate electricity from ocean currents, which are 800x denser than air. They operate in predictable patterns, unlike wind or solar.
learn.c2_text: Turbines are submerged, with rotors designed for low-speed, high-torque output. They can generate 1-2 MW per unit, with minimal environmental impact.
learn.c3_text: India's Andaman Sea and Gulf of Kutch have strong tidal currents. The government is exploring pilot projects, aiming to generate 500 MW by 2030.
learn.stat3: Tidal turbine cost: $1.5-2.5 million per MW

learn.title: Lithium-Ion Batteries
learn.intro: Lithium-ion batteries store excess renewable energy, stabilizing grids and enabling electric vehicles. Their efficiency and scalability make them essential for energy transition.
learn.c1_text: Lithium-ion batteries store energy via chemical reactions, releasing it as electricity. They charge and discharge efficiently, supporting solar and wind integration.
learn.c2_text: Batteries provide frequency regulation, peak shaving, and backup power. A 100 MW battery can power 10,000 homes for 4 hours, balancing grid demand.
learn.c3_text: India's battery storage capacity is expected to reach 10 GW by 2030. The government offers subsidies for grid-scale projects, supporting renewable energy integration.

learn.title: Offshore Wind Farms
learn.intro: Offshore wind farms harness stronger coastal winds, offering higher energy output. Their design and grid connection require advanced engineering and policy support.
learn.c1_text: Offshore turbines are larger, with 10-12 MW capacity. Installation involves floating platforms and subsea cables, requiring specialized vessels and expertise.
learn.c2_text: Corrosion, saltwater, and weather conditions demand durable materials. Grid connection requires high-voltage direct current (HVDC) for efficient transmission.
learn.c3_text: India's first offshore wind farm in Gujarat has 2.5 MW capacity. The government aims for 30 GW by 2030, with policy incentives for private investment.
learn.stat3: Offshore turbine cost: $2.5-3.5 million per MW
